channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Escalating maritime tensions challenge fragile Iran–U.S. ceasefire in Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Tensions between Iran and the United States are intensifying again despite an existing ceasefire, as new incidents unfold in the strategically critical Strait of Hormuz. According to reports, Iranian ...

item.title: Alberta Premier to provide update on fall referendum question
item.description: Alberta Premier Danielle Smith is preparing to give an update regarding the government's plan for the fall referendum question. While specific details have not yet been publicly disclosed, the announc...

item.title: Cape Town intensifies conservation efforts for critically endangered African penguins
item.description: The City of Cape Town is commemorating World Penguin Day on Saturday, April 25, with an urgent call to action regarding the African penguin, now classified as Critically Endangered. Following a 2024 u...

item.title: 695 Police Officers Transferred from Lagos Zone 2 Amid Allegations of Poor Welfare and Unpaid Allowances
item.description: In a major shake-up within the Nigeria Police Force, 695 officers have been transferred from Zone 2 Command in Lagos to various locations across the country. The move, led by former Lagos CP AIG Jimoh...

item.title: Trump Announces Invitation for Netanyahu and Aoun to Washington for Talks
item.description: In a development amid ongoing Middle East tensions, U.S. President Donald Trump announced that he will invite Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu and Lebanese President Joseph Aoun to Washington...

item.title: Researchers Discover Fast16 Malware That Could Have Sabotaged Iran’s Nuclear Efforts Before Stuxnet
item.description: Newly discovered malware known as Fast16, which dates back to 2005, may have been used in an early cyberattack against Iran's nuclear program, possibly predating the infamous Stuxnet attack. Researche...

item.title: Former business partner faces court over alleged harassment via emails
item.description: Simon Lyons, a 45-year-old investment executive, appeared at Southwark Crown Court accused of harassing his former business partner, Farid Alizadeh, and Alizadeh's wife, Natasha. The pair co-founded E...

item.title: Congress Challenges EC Notice to Kharge Over His Remarks on PM Modi
item.description: On 23 April 2026, the Congress party submitted a summary response to the Election Commission's notice issued to its president, Mallikarjun Kharge, regarding his controversial 'terrorist' remark. The p...

item.title: Ferré Gola to Make Johannesburg Debut with Live Acoustic Show in May
item.description: South African music fans can look forward to a major live performance as celebrated Congolese artist Ferré Gola prepares for his first-ever concert in Johannesburg. The event is scheduled for Saturday...

item.title: 10-year-old Texas boy charged with murder following fatal stabbing of 13-year-old girl
item.description: A tragic incident in Houston, Texas, has resulted in the death of 13-year-old Riley DeArmas after a confrontation involving multiple young people. A 10-year-old boy, who was present during the inciden...

item.title: Trump Demands Apology from Schumer Over Border Patrol and ICE Comments
item.description: President Donald Trump publicly criticized Senate Minority Leader Chuck Schumer after Schumer claimed that 'nobody respects' Border Patrol and Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE). Trump called t...

item.title: Critique of Australian media coverage of electric vehicles during the petrol price surge
item.description: In this opinion piece, John Birmingham takes aim at recent Australian media coverage of electric vehicles (EVs), particularly singling out outlets like the ABC and Nine for what he sees as poor-qualit...

item.title: Kenyan Man Known for Resembling Jomo Kenyatta Returns from US and Joins Fuel Price Protests
item.description: Michael Ngige, a Kenyan man widely known for closely resembling Kenya’s founding president Jomo Kenyatta, has returned to the country after spending 14 years in the United States. His return has drawn...

item.title: Understanding the Factors Behind Southeastern U.S. Wildfires Amid Severe Drought
item.description: Large parts of the southeastern United States, particularly Georgia and Florida, are experiencing severe wildfires driven by a combination of prolonged drought, dry fuels, and strong winds. Since July...

item.title: Joyce Wangari from Ngong seeks biological father in Kitale after 32 years and shares painful experience with hostile stepfather
item.description: Joyce Wangari, a young woman from Ngong in Kenya, is longing to meet her biological father who left her mother 32 years ago. She has never set eyes on him and grew up under the care of her maternal gr...

item.title: NCRPO downplays concerns of rising crimes due to oil crisis
item.description: The National Capital Region Police Office (NCRPO) has clarified that there is no significant surge in crime, despite growing concerns that the ongoing energy crisis might be fueling criminal activitie...

item.title: Ladakh's New Tourism Reforms Aim to Boost Ease of Doing Business
item.description: In a bid to simplify the tourism sector in Ladakh, the government has introduced sweeping reforms to encourage ease of doing business. The Lieutenant Governor, VK Saxena, announced that these reforms ...

item.title: Colombia Hosts Global Summit to Phase Out Fossil Fuels Amid Climate Crisis
item.description: More than 50 countries have gathered in Santa Marta, Colombia, for a key conference aimed at transitioning away from fossil fuels. This event, which runs from April 24 to 29, follows the disappointmen...

item.title: Woman found not criminally responsible for killing her infant is granted absolute discharge by Ontario Review Board
item.description: A woman from Kitchener, Ontario, has been granted an absolute discharge by the Ontario Review Board more than five years after she stabbed her eight‑month‑old son to death during a major psychotic epi...

item.title: Australia's eSafety Commission issues warning to gaming platforms over child safety risks
item.description: Australia's eSafety Commissioner has taken action against major gaming platforms like Roblox, Fortnite, and Steam over concerns about child grooming and online radicalisation. These platforms, popular...
